rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MORTGAGE LENDER SERVICES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 31, 2023, line 1. Hearing Re: Surplus Funds Proceeds is continued to August 1, 2023. No later than July 21, 2023 parties to file supplemental briefs (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing the effect of the May 4, 2023 sale on their claims to the surplus funds. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLA VOLPI ET AL VS. DESIGN LINE CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records For Defendant Design Line Construction
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 31, 2023, line 5. Defendant's Motion to Quash Subpoenas is DENIED as follows: 1. The parties enter into a standard protective order relating to disclosure of these documents and shall be made part of the order. Parties to meet and confer on the form of the order and Plaintiff shall present it for execution at the hearing. 2. Each of the third parties whose records were subpoenaed shall produce within 15 days of the filing of the orders all documents that have been subpoenaed to defense counsel the documents for a first look. Defense counsel shall maintain the originals of all these documents so that any further disputes, if any, regarding these documents can be resolved without involving the third parties. 3. Within 15 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to DLC, defense counsel shall produce all documents relating to DLC except they may redact portions subject to a privilege log as to third party privacy interests. 4. Within 15 days of defense counsel's receipt of the documents related to the McNabbs, defense counsel shall produce all documents except those that they considered privileged under the right of privacy and/or redact privileged portions subject to a privilege log. At a minimum they shall produce the periodic statements from each bank. 5. All of this is without prejudice to Plaintiffs making subsequent requests to defendants for production of documents or removal or redactions based on good cause. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)
